title: ACH Origination Anomalies
spl:
index=payments sourcetype="ach:origination" sec_code="WEB"
| stats sum(amount) as batch_total, dc(individual_id) as distinct_receivers by company_id, file_id
| eventstats avg(batch_total) as avg_batch by company_id
| where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500
| sort -batch_total
description: Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.
value: Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.
implementation: Validate company_id against registered originators; alert on new SEC codes or return spikes after origination.
visualization: Table (suspicious batches), Line chart (ACH volume by company), Bar chart (returns vs. originations).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: ACH/NACHA ingestion, treasury workstation.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="ach:origination"`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Validate company_id against registered originators; alert on new SEC codes or return spikes after origination.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="ach:origination" sec_code="WEB"
| stats sum(amount) as batch_total, dc(individual_id) as distinct_receivers by company_id, file_id
| eventstats avg(batch_total) as avg_batch by company_id
| where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500
| sort -batch_total
```

Understanding this SPL

**ACH Origination Anomalies** ‚Äî Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="ach:origination"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ACH/NACHA ingestion, treasury workstation. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: ach:origination. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="ach:origination". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by company_id, file_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by company_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (suspicious batches), Line chart (ACH volume by company), Bar chart (returns vs. originations).
grandmaExplanation: Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational err ‚Äî so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
